title: The Marketing Meeting
summary: Michael Donovan and Vinnie Micciche discussed LLMs, web access, and bot traffic. Vinnie emphasized dealer-centric messaging, rapid development, and consistent content, highlighting data as a unique advantage. Participants agreed to accelerate development, improve content for lead generation, and prioritize features aligning with Vinnie's framework.

decisions.description: Accelerate development and improve content for lead generation.
Prioritize features aligning with people, dealers, customer experience.
Driving leads is the paramount goal for the marketing team.
Maintain brand consistency with current colors and logos.
Source of leads doesn't matter, only that they are received.

actionItems.task: Put together revised social media posts.
Find and send research on lead origins to the group.
Review and provide feedback on updated creatives.
Put together outline material for Vinnie Micciche.
Improve document view for full post, caption, and hashtags.

tensions: Vinnie Micciche's concern about the future of digital retailing due to LLMs.
Vinnie Micciche's frustration with slow development pace and planning.
Vinnie Micciche feeling his dealer input is 'listening, but not hearing'.
Vinnie Micciche pushing back on Michael Donovan's 'dashboard' messaging.
Vinnie Micciche's concern about a dealer switching chat providers and lead loss.
referencesToPast: Matt Watson noted bot traffic development occurred six months prior.
Michael Donovan discussed ongoing social media content strategy.
Michael Donovan shared past experience with UMDO dashboard adoption.
Vinnie Micciche stressed brand consistency, established for 12 years.
Michael Donovan commented on ongoing WordPress site technical issues.
Michael Donovan clarified Vinnie Micciche was reading an outdated document.

contributions.description: Explored LLMs beyond ChatGPT for current web information.
Reported bots now dominate internet traffic for LLM scraping.
Emphasized crucial role of dealer-centric messaging and content.
Questioned future of digital retailing given LLM capabilities.
Advocated for faster development pace, leveraging company data.
